item.title: Oneohtrix Point Never’s Sense of the Uncanny
item.description: Also: Sarah Larson’s latest podcast picks, “The Rocky Horror Show” and “The Balusters” on Broadway, the French singer Oklou, and more.

item.title: Cory Doctorow on the High Cost of Living with the Ultra-Rich
item.description: The writer and internet critic discusses books that reflect different facets of living in a society run by billionaires.

item.title: Donald Trump’s Spring Cleaning
item.description: The exact reasons are often left vague and the successors to be determined, but people are leaving the Administration—including three Cabinet secretaries.
item.dc:creator: ByBenjamin Wallace-Wells

item.title: Reverend Billy Takes On Norman Foster’s New Monolith
item.description: Fresh from opening shows for Neil Young, the street preacher Billy Talen has moved on from burning Mickey Mouse in effigy to protesting JPMorgan Chase’s ties to fossil fuels.
item.dc:creator: ByJake Offenhartz

item.title: Medallions, Movement, and Mamdani at MOMA PS1
item.description: The cab-driving Elcharfa brothers, Bilal and Salah, star in a new piece by the artist Kenneth Tam that showcases the hardships of their jobs.
item.dc:creator: ByEmma Allen

item.title: Patrick Ball’s Path to Broadway and “Becky Shaw”
item.description: Before “The Pitt,” the actor waited tables, made lattes, and schlepped Carrie Bradshaw’s wardrobe around town.
item.dc:creator: ByRachel Syme

item.title: Ellen Burstyn’s Inner Library
item.description: Kris Kristofferson told her he was a poet when they co-starred in “Alice Doesn’t Live Here Anymore.” Her new book tells the story of her life in poetry.
item.dc:creator: ByMichael Schulman

item.title: Can the E.P.A. Survive Lee Zeldin?
item.description: The agency, which was founded to protect the environment and human health, has cancelled safety regulations, supported coal, and stopped caring about climate change.
item.dc:creator: ByElizabeth Kolbert

item.title: What Happens When Someone Throws a Message in a Bottle Into the Sea?
item.description: Most simply disappear. One enthusiast is on a quest to find the notes—and the people behind them.
item.dc:creator: ByLauren Collins

item.title: Donald Trump’s Pardon Economy
item.description: For some wealthy offenders, clemency is just a golf game—or a million-dollar plate at Mar-a-Lago—away.
item.dc:creator: ByRuth Marcus

item.title: Laurie Metcalf’s Third Act
item.description: The once cancelled producer Scott Rudin has staked his own comeback on making her the First Lady of American Theatre.
item.dc:creator: ByMichael Schulman

item.title: Ava’s Life List
item.description: Spring is here, and with it sightings of the Great-Breasted Hausfrau, the Potbellied Galoot, and the Common Nanny.
item.dc:creator: ByGarry Trudeau

item.title: “Process of Elimination”
item.description: The night the tip jar went missing, we assumed that it had been stolen by a student, or maybe a professor—an adjunct—who had taken it when we weren’t looking.
item.dc:creator: BySaïd Sayrafiezadeh

item.title: The Many Forms of Marcel Duchamp
item.description: How the shape-shifting artist radicalized art itself.
item.dc:creator: ByHilton Als

item.title: A German Master’s Modernist Epic of Postwar Amnesia and Hypocrisy
item.description: Wolfgang Koeppen’s “trilogy of failure,” written from 1951 to 1954, is a sprawling, polyphonic portrait of a physically and morally shattered country.
item.dc:creator: ByBecca Rothfeld

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: “In Search of Now,” “Nothing Random,” “Of Loss and Lavender,” and “No Way Home.”

item.title: The Sqirl Redemption Arc
item.description: The beloved L.A. café was brought low by a bucket of moldy jam. Now it’s open for dinner.
item.dc:creator: ByHannah Goldfield

item.title: How Long Can Martha Graham’s Dance Revolution Last?
item.description: As the company she left behind celebrates its centenary, it finds itself caught between preservation and radical tradition.
item.dc:creator: ByJennifer Homans

item.title: “Half Man” Tests the Limits of Brotherly Love
item.description: Richard Gadd’s follow-up to “Baby Reindeer” traces a decades-long quasi-familial relationship that’s thornier than any other male bond on TV.
item.dc:creator: ByInkoo Kang

item.title: “Tompkins Square”
item.description: “It was an evening they had planned, privately, in the sequester / of their thoughts for years before it could or should have / happened.”
item.dc:creator: ByAnthony Walton

item.title: “A Theory on the Origin of Language”
item.description: “Last night, after months away from home, / a lapwing piercing the still dark still / with its warnings.”
item.dc:creator: ByTishani Doshi

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the May 4, 2026, magazine.

item.title: The Crossword: Monday, April 27, 2026
item.description: A challenging puzzle.
item.dc:creator: ByWill Nediger
